results.title: How to make AI ads that look real and convert | Cory Dobbin posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content:
Revolutionize your video marketing with AI-powered scoring
I built an AI system that predicts video ad performance before launch.
Here's what I learned:
The secret lies in pattern recognition:
1. Historical performance data
- Views to conversion ratio
- Audience retention curves
- Engagement patterns
- Click-through rates
2. Visual elements analysis
- Color psychology impact
- Scene transition timing
- Brand element placement
- Motion flow scoring
3. Audio optimization
- Voice modulation effects
- Background music impact
- Sound-visual sync rating
- Silent viewing effectiveness
Key implementation steps:
1. Collect 50+ past video performances
2. Train AI on success patterns
3. Create scoring parameters
4. Test with new content
5. Refine based on results
Real results: [...] Conclusion:
Great AI ads come from mastering every step of the creative chain... which all starts and ends with better inputs. When you do that, AI becomes FAR more than just a shortcut. It becomes a real competitive advantage that you can rely on predictably and consistently.
Now, go out there and test.
See you at the forefront. [...] The result?
- Faster testing of creative concepts
- Reduced wasted ad spend
- Thousands of ad variations at pennies per asset
- Full ownership of creative assets
Tip: Combine AI speed with human strategy. AI generates the volume and experimentation, humans guide messaging and storytelling. That’s how the top-performing brands consistently beat the 80% failure rate for ad creative.
The takeaway: The future of ad creative isn’t about manual design or guessing. It’s about automating experimentation, scaling what works, and keeping brand consistency intact.
Want to see how AI-generated ads could work for your brand? Comment “AI Ads” and we’ll show you a live demo.
#nanobanana #ads

results.title: AI-Driven Advertising for Conversion Optimization
results.content:
### E-commerce Conversion Success: Sephora Virtual Artist

Challenge: Sephora needed to provide personalized product recommendations at scale across their digital channels, helping customers find the right products without overwhelming them with choices, ultimately driving more conversions.

Approach: They implemented AI-powered virtual try-on technology that analyzes facial features, skin tone, and customer preferences to recommend products and show realistic previews of how makeup would look, optimized for conversion.

Results: 30% increase in online sales and significantly higher engagement rates. The AI identified patterns like customers who use virtual try-on features being 40% more likely to purchase within 48 hours. [...] Understanding how AI advertising actually functions for conversion optimization helps you make better strategic decisions and set realistic expectations. The process happens in four continuous phases that work together to improve your conversion performance over time.

Data Collection Phase: AI systems gather information from multiple sources – customer behavior on your website, demographic data, purchase history, social media interactions, and even external factors like seasonality and market trends. For Facebook campaigns, this includes pixel data, customer lists, and engagement patterns across all your ads, with special focus on conversion events. [...] Here's where AI gets really impressive for conversion optimization – it doesn't just look at basic demographics like age and location. We're talking about analyzing over 1,000 data points per user to understand purchase intent, behavior patterns, and optimal timing for conversions. This level of granular analysis is impossible for human marketers to replicate at scale.

Take Sephora's approach with their Virtual Artist AI tool. By analyzing customer behavior, purchase history, and even facial recognition data from their app, they delivered personalized product recommendations that resulted in a 30% increase in online sales. The AI identified patterns like "customers who try virtual lipstick on Tuesday evenings are 40% more likely to purchase within 48 hours." Pretty specific, right?

They open ChatGPT, type a vague prompt, get a decent-looking image, launch it, and then they wonder why it doesn't convert. But the reality is that if the message is weak, the ad won't convert no matter how good it looks. The image gets the click, the message makes the sale. Most people are only focusing on one of those. Before I generate a single ad, I use Claude to build what I call a product identity brief. I give it a prompt like this: "Analyze this brand and build me a complete product identity brief." Within 30 seconds, Claude builds a full strategic breakdown with customer personas, pain points, buying triggers, and even more. I'll drop the exact prompt in the description below. Now, here's why this [...] exact prompt in the description below. Now, here's why this matters. Instead of creating random AI ads, you're creating ads based on actual customer psychology. That shift alone changes everything about your ads quality. Now, we move into the actual ad creation, and that is where I use Superscale. Think of it as an AI ad creation agent. I create a new brand based in the Shopify store's URL. For this example, I'm using Carpe, a deodorant brand doing over $1 million a month. After I add the store's URL, Super Skill starts analyzing the brand, the URL, and even the competitors. And on the first run, it already pulled Carpe's product directly into the workspace. But here's where most people mess up. They give the AI almost zero context, and then they wonder why the ads feel generic. So what I

Platforms like Mintly and Amazon Video Generator make this easy. Mintly’s AI Static Ad Maker then lets you select proven ad templates by cloning top ads from the Meta Ad Library—meaning you start with ads that already convert well. Next, the AI generates copy and creative assets tailored to your product.

AdvertiseAi even adds performance prediction, so you know which ads are likely to work best. Here’s a tip: combine static ads that take 15-30 seconds to create with short video ads (2-8 minutes) for maximum impressions and engagement. Don’t skip performance prediction or ignore AI’s targeting suggestions—doing so can reduce your ad’s effectiveness. [...] That’s where an AI ad creator shines. Tools like Mintly analyze your product images and use Meta Ad Library cloning to recreate winning ad templates from the biggest brands in under a minute. Imagine picking a top-performing ad layout and swapping in your product—logo and labels intact—without starting from scratch.

This speeds up your ad creation and keeps your brand looking authentic. Plus, platforms like AdvertiseAi combine AI copywriting with visuals and even predict how well your ads will perform, boosting your click-through rates and ROAS. But be careful: over-customizing AI-generated templates without testing can kill the performance benefits. [...] Q: Why use Mintly over other AI ad makers?

A: Mintly outperforms generic AI generators by letting you clone proven ads from the biggest brands in under 60 seconds while keeping your product’s logos and labels exactly as they are. This means your ads look authentic and convert better. Plus, at about $0.20 per ad versus $20+ elsewhere, Mintly pays for itself quickly.
